Meet Our Physicians

Compassionate Expertise in Rheumatology

Two board-certified rheumatologists dedicated to delivering specialist-level care across the West Valley.

Dr. Viji Mahadevan
MD • Rheumatologist
ABIM Certified ACR Member AZ Licensed Research Faculty

Dr. Viji Mahadevan has practised in the Valley for the past 9 years. She completed her Internal Medicine residency in Pittsburgh where she won the “Best Resident” award 3 years in a row. She subsequently graduated from the prestigious National Institutes of Health in Maryland where she completed her Rheumatology fellowship in 2004.

She is Board Certified in Internal Medicine and Rheumatology. She has a strong research background in Rheumatoid Arthritis (RA) and has presented her research findings at the national rheumatology meetings. Before moving to the Valley in 2007, she was in private practice in Virginia.

She has spoken at several community events to help educate patients about arthritis and osteoporosis. She is actively involved in arthritis research trials. She brings with her several years of experience combined with her passion for providing a personalised and compassionate approach to caring for patients with arthritis.

She lives in Glendale with her husband and twin boys. She enjoys yoga, reading and spending time with her family.

Dr. Rajbir Gulati
MD • Rheumatologist
ABIM Certified ACR Member AZ Licensed

Dr. Rajbir Gulati grew up in Chandigarh, India. She graduated at the top of her class from Government Medical College in Chandigarh. She completed her residency in Internal Medicine at Saint Francis Hospital, Evanston, IL. She was fascinated with new research and clinical development in rheumatology and decided to pursue a career in the field.

She went on to complete her fellowship in Rheumatology from the prestigious Cedars Sinai Medical Center in Los Angeles, CA. After completing her fellowship she moved to the Valley with her family and has been practicing rheumatology since 2014. She is board certified in Internal Medicine and Rheumatology.

Dr. Gulati has a philosophy of treating every patient like her own family. She feels that the doctor’s responsibility is not only to treat the disease but also to understand the patient’s emotional and psychological reaction to it. She understands the long-term and complex needs of each patient in order to lead a healthy and productive life.

She lives in Glendale with her husband and three young boys. She enjoys travelling and spending time with her family and friends.

Shared Credentials & Practice Highlights
Board Certified in Internal Medicine & Rheumatology (ABIM)
Active Members, American College of Rheumatology (ACR)
In-office biologic infusion therapy suite
Licensed to practice in the State of Arizona
Actively involved in arthritis research trials
What We Treat

Conditions We Specialize In

We diagnose and manage the full range of rheumatologic and autoimmune conditions, from common arthritis to complex systemic disease.

1 • Rheumatoid Arthritis

Autoimmune inflammatory arthritis causing progressive joint damage. Early biologic therapy prevents long-term disability.

2 • Osteoarthritis

Degenerative joint disease of hips, knees, hands, and spine. Expert pain management and joint-preservation strategies.

3 • Psoriatic Arthritis

Inflammatory arthritis linked to psoriasis. Modern biologic and small-molecule therapies achieve lasting remission.

4 • Gout & Pseudogout

Crystal-induced arthritis causing sudden, severe joint pain. Highly treatable with proper urate-lowering therapy.

5 • Lupus (SLE)

Systemic autoimmune disease affecting skin, joints, kidneys, and more. Specialist-level monitoring and tailored therapy.

6 • Fibromyalgia

Widespread musculoskeletal pain with fatigue and sleep disruption. Comprehensive, evidence-based management plans.

7 • Ankylosing Spondylitis

Inflammatory arthritis of the spine causing chronic back pain. Biologic therapies have transformed patient outcomes.

8 • Sjögren’s Syndrome

Autoimmune exocrine gland disease causing dry eyes and mouth. Systemic complication monitoring and targeted relief.

9 • Osteoporosis

Bone density loss increasing fracture risk. DEXA screening, prevention counseling, and FDA-approved treatment plans.

10 • Vasculitis

Inflammatory disease of blood vessel walls requiring prompt immunosuppressive therapy and specialist follow-up.

11 • Inflammatory Myositis

Muscle inflammation causing proximal weakness. Expert workup for polymyositis, dermatomyositis, and IBM.

12 • Raynaud’s Phenomenon

Exaggerated cold-induced vasospasm reducing blood flow to extremities. Often signals connective tissue disease.

Learn More
Quick Self-Assessment

Should You See a Rheumatologist?

Answer three quick questions to see if a rheumatology consultation is right for you.

Question 1 of 3
Have you had joint pain, swelling, or stiffness lasting more than 6 weeks?
Question 2 of 3
Do your symptoms include morning stiffness that lasts more than 30 minutes?
Question 3 of 3
Have you experienced fatigue, skin rashes, dry eyes, or other symptoms alongside joint pain?
✓
A Rheumatology Consultation is Recommended

Based on your answers, your symptoms are consistent with an inflammatory or autoimmune condition that warrants evaluation by a rheumatologist. Early diagnosis and treatment can prevent long-term joint damage.

Patient Education

Learn About Your Health

Evidence-based articles written for patients managing arthritis and autoimmune conditions.

Biologic Therapy
What to Expect from Your First Biologic Treatment
Biologics have transformed outcomes for RA and other inflammatory conditions. Here’s how to prepare, what side effects to watch for, and how to know if treatment is working.
Read Article →
Lifestyle
Gout Management: Diet, Hydration, and When to Seek Care
Gout attacks are intensely painful but largely preventable. Learn which foods raise uric acid, how hydration helps, and when medication is the right choice.
Read Article →
Osteoporosis
Bone Density Testing: Who Needs a DEXA Scan and When
Osteoporosis is often called a “silent disease” because bone loss has no symptoms until a fracture occurs. Here’s who should be screened and what treatment options exist.
Read Article →
Common Questions

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know before your visit.

A referral is preferred but not required for most patients. Some insurance plans, however, do require a primary care referral for specialist visits. Please check with your insurance provider beforehand. If your PCP has already recommended seeing a rheumatologist, please have them send records to help us prepare for your visit.

Please bring your insurance card and photo ID, a list of all current medications and supplements, any recent lab results or imaging reports, and your completed new patient forms (available to download from the Patient Resources section above). Arriving 15 minutes early is recommended.

New patient consultations are typically 60–75 minutes. This allows time for a thorough medical history review, physical examination, and a detailed discussion of your symptoms and treatment options. Please plan your schedule accordingly and avoid booking appointments immediately after.

Biologics are advanced medications that target specific parts of the immune system driving inflammation. They are used when conventional DMARDs (like methotrexate) have not provided adequate control. Not every patient needs biologics—treatment decisions are individualized based on disease severity, prior therapy response, and your overall health.

Yes. Our in-office infusion suite allows patients to receive IV biologic therapies (such as Remicade, Orencia, Rituxan, and others) in a comfortable, monitored setting. Infusion appointments are scheduled separately and typically take 2–4 hours depending on the medication.

Telehealth follow-up appointments are available for established patients with stable conditions. New patient consultations and physical examinations must be done in person. Please call our office to determine if your upcoming visit qualifies for telehealth.

The fastest way to request a refill is through the Patient Portal. Alternatively, you can call our office directly at (623) 219-4040. Please allow 2–3 business days for refill processing. Note that some medications require a recent visit or lab work before refills can be authorized.

We ask that you provide at least 24 hours’ notice to cancel or reschedule an appointment. This allows us to offer your slot to another patient in need. Late cancellations or no-shows may be subject to a fee. If you are running late, please call us as soon as possible.
